page_title: "phare Provider"
description: |-
  The Phare provider provides resources to interact with [Phare](https://phare.io), a modern uptime monitoring and incident management platform.

# Phare Provider

The Phare provider allows you to manage Phare resources using Terraform. This provider supports creating and managing projects, uptime monitors, status pages, alert rules, and more.

## Authentication

The provider requires an API key for authentication. There are two types of API keys:

### API Key Types

1. **Organization-scoped API keys** (`pha_org_*`)
   - Can create and manage projects
   - Require `project_scope` to be specified for most resources
   - Can manage resources across multiple projects

2. **Project-scoped API keys** (`pha_*`)
   - Limited to a specific project
   - Do not require `project_scope` configuration
   - Cannot create or delete projects

You can learn more about [API key scopes](https://docs.phare.io/api-reference/introduction#choosing-the-right-scope) in their dedicated documentation.

### Configuration Methods

You can configure the API key in several ways:

1. **Provider configuration**

```hcl
provider "phare" {
  api_key = "your-api-key-here"
}
```

2. **Terraform variable**

```hcl
variable "phare_api_key" {
  sensitive = true
}

provider "phare" {
  api_key = var.phare_api_key
}
```

3. **Environment variable**

```bash
export PHARE_API_KEY="your-api-key-here"
```

## Provider Configuration

### Basic Configuration

```hcl
terraform {
  required_providers {
    phare = {
      source  = "phare/phare"
      version = ">= 0.0.1"
    }
  }
}

provider "phare" {
  api_key = "your-api-key-here"
}
```

### Advanced Configuration

```hcl
provider "phare" {
  api_key = "your-api-key-here"

  # Optional: Override the default API base URL
  base_url = "https://api.phare.io"

  # Optional: HTTP client timeout in seconds (default: 30)
  timeout = 60

  # Optional: When using an organization-scoped API key you can set
  # a global project scope that will be used on all resources
  project_scope = "your-project-slug"
}
```

### Environment Variables

The provider supports the following environment variables:

- `PHARE_API_KEY` - API key for authentication
- `PHARE_BASE_URL` - Base API URL (defaults to `https://api.phare.io`)
- `PHARE_PROJECT_ID` - Project ID for organization-scoped API keys
- `PHARE_PROJECT_SLUG` - Project slug for organization-scoped API keys

## Project Scope

When using an **organization-scoped API key**, you must specify a project scope for most resources. The project scope can be configured in several ways:

### Provider-level Project Scope

```hcl
provider "phare" {
  api_key = "pha_org_your-organization-key"
  project_scope = "your-project-slug"  # or 123 for project ID
}
```

### Resource-level Project Scope

You can override the provider-level project scope for specific resources:

```hcl
resource "phare_uptime_monitor_http" "website" {
  project_scope = 123  # This will overrides provider-level scope

  name = "Website Monitor"
  # ... other configuration
}
```

### Environment Variables for Project Scope

```bash
# Set project scope via environment variables
export PHARE_PROJECT_ID="123"
# or
export PHARE_PROJECT_SLUG="your-project-slug"
```

> **Note**: Project-scoped API keys (`pha_*`) do not require a project scope configuration.

## Example Usage

```terraform
terraform {
  required_providers {
    phare = {
      source  = "phare/phare"
      version = "~> 0.0.1"
    }
  }
}

# Set this variable in a *.tfvars file
# or define a PHARE_API_KEY environment variable instead
variable "phare_api_key" {
  sensitive = true
}

# Configure the Phare Provider
provider "phare" {
  api_key = "your-api-key-here"

  # Optional: Override the default API base URL
  base_url = "https://api.phare.io"

  # Optional: HTTP client timeout in seconds (default: 30)
  timeout = 30

  # Optional: When using an organization-scoped API key you can set
  # a global project scope that will be used on all resources
  project_scope = "your-project-slug"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `api_key` (String, Sensitive) Phare API key for authentication. Can also be set via PHARE_API_KEY environment variable.
- `base_url` (String) Phare API base URL. Defaults to https://api.phare.io. Can be overridden via PHARE_BASE_URL environment variable for testing.
- `project_scope` (Dynamic) Optional. Project scope for API requests when using an organization-scoped API key. Accepts either a numeric project ID (e.g., 123) or a string project slug (e.g., "my-project"). Can also be set via PHARE_PROJECT_ID or PHARE_PROJECT_SLUG environment variables.
- `timeout` (Number) HTTP client timeout in seconds. Defaults to 30.




## Support

For issues with the provider, please:

1. Check the [Phare documentation](https://docs.phare.io)
2. Review the [Terraform provider documentation](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/phare/phare/latest/docs)
3. Open an issue on the provider repository
